audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for authority dispute, vineyard tenants, cornerstone, Caesar/image, resurrection dispute, David/Lord question, and warning against scribes.

verses.restored: It happened on one of the days, as he was teaching the people in the temple and announcing good news, the chief priests and scribes with the elders stood near.
They spoke, saying to him, Tell us by what authority you do these things, or who is the one who gave you this authority?
Answering, he said to them, I also will ask you a word; tell me:
The baptism of John, was it from heaven or from humans?
They reasoned together, saying, If we say, From heaven, he will say, Why did you not trust him?
But if we say, From humans, all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded John was a prophet.
They answered that they did not know from where.
Jesus said to them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.
He began to speak this parable to the people: A person planted a vineyard, leased it to farmers, and went away for considerable times.
At the season he sent a servant to the farmers, so that they would give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But the farmers beat him and sent him away empty.
He sent another servant; but they also beat and dishonored that one and sent him away empty.
He sent a third; but they also wounded this one and threw him out.
The lord of the vineyard said, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect this one.
Seeing him, the farmers reasoned with one another, saying, This is the heir; let us kill him, so that the inheritance may become ours.
Throwing him outside the vineyard, they killed him. What then will the lord of the vineyard do to them?
He will come and destroy these farmers and give the vineyard to others. Hearing this, they said, May it not happen.
But looking at them, he said, What then is this that has been written: The stone the builders rejected, this became the head of the corner?
Everyone falling on that stone will be broken; but on whomever it falls, it will crush that one.
The scribes and chief priests sought to lay hands on him in that hour, yet they feared the people, for they knew he spoke this parable against them.
Watching carefully, they sent spies pretending themselves to be righteous, so that they might catch him in word, to hand him over to the rule and authority of the governor.
They questioned him, saying, Teacher, we know that you speak and teach rightly and do not receive face, but teach the way of the Divine in truth.
Is it lawful for us to give tribute to Caesar, or not?
Perceiving their craftiness, he said to them,
Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription does it have? They said, Caesar's.
He said to them, Then give back the things of Caesar to Caesar, and the things of the Divine to the Divine.
They were not able to catch the word before the people; marveling at his answer, they were silent.
Some Sadducees, those denying there is a resurrection, came and questioned him,
saying, Teacher, Moses wrote for us: If someone's brother dies having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should take the wife and raise up seed for his brother.
There were seven brothers. The first, taking a wife, died childless.
The second
and the third took her; likewise also the seven left no children and died.
Last, the woman also died.
Therefore, in the resurrection, of which of them does she become wife? For the seven had her as wife.
Jesus said to them, The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage.
But those considered worthy to attain that age and the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.
For neither are they able to die anymore, for they are equal to messengers and are sons of the Divine, being sons of resurrection.
That the dead are raised, even Moses showed at the bush, when he calls the Lord the Divine of Abraham, Divine of Isaac, and Divine of Jacob.
He is not Divine of dead ones but of living ones, for all live to him.
Some of the scribes answered, Teacher, you spoke beautifully/well.
For they no longer dared to question him anything.
He said to them, How do they say the Messiah is David's son?
For David himself says in the scroll of Psalms: The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand,
until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.
David therefore calls him Lord; so how is he his son?
While all the people were hearing, he said to the disciples,
Watch yourselves from the scribes who desire to walk around in long robes, love greetings in the marketplaces, first seats in the synagogues, and first places at dinners,
who devour widows' houses and for appearance make long prayers. These will receive greater judgment.
